boundaryNotes: Used only the local backend runtime on 8080 and evidence from narrow backend tests.
Validation writes occurred only through repo tests that target the dedicated opentoggl_test schema.

assertions.title: Running timer conflict rule is fixed across entry points

steps.action: Review validation contract, mission guidance, regressions test coverage, and prior scrutiny for supported timer-start entry points.
steps.expected: Evidence should show every supported start entry point enforces the same fixed conflict result.
steps.observed: Current code and prior scrutiny show only CreateTimeEntry is proven as a conflicting second-start path; UpdateTimeEntry preserves existing stop when Stop=nil and the current test only updates the already-running entry.

steps.action: Run go test ./apps/backend/internal/tracking/application -run 'TestRunningTimerConflictOn(CreateEntryPoint|UpdateEntryPoint)' -count=1 -v with Postgres access.
steps.expected: Narrow tests should prove conflict handling across all supported start entry points.
steps.observed: Both tests passed, but TestRunningTimerConflictOnUpdateEntryPoint only proves same-entry running update succeeds; it does not prove a second conflicting start entry point.

steps.action: Check local runtime reachability with curl against /api/v9/me/time_entries/current.
steps.expected: Runtime should be reachable for supplemental backend evidence.
steps.observed: Runtime responded 403 for invalid credentials, confirming reachability only; no validator-owned runtime account details were available for deeper API replay.

commands: psql -Atqc "select schema_name from information_schema.schemata where schema_name like 'opentoggl_test%' order by schema_name;"
evidence.runtime: GET /api/v9/me/time_entries/current -> 403 with invalid credentials (reachability proof only)
evidence.schema: Database opentoggl contains dedicated schema opentoggl_test

assertions.issues: Blocked by contract/coverage gap: current repository evidence does not prove a second conflicting timer-start entry point beyond CreateTimeEntry, and prior scrutiny explicitly marks VAL-REG-001 unresolved on that basis.

assertions.title: Historical entries survive related object lifecycle changes

steps.action: Review regression tests for project archival and member disable behavior.
steps.expected: Coverage should preserve historical entry visibility after related project/member lifecycle changes.

steps.action: Run go test ./apps/backend/internal/tracking/application -run 'TestHistoricalEntriesSurvive(ProjectArchival|MemberDisable)' -count=1 -v against Postgres-backed tests.
steps.expected: Both lifecycle regressions should pass with real backend persistence.
steps.observed: Both tests passed, proving direct readback and list visibility persist after project archival and member disable.

steps.action: Inspect PostgreSQL schemas for dedicated test schema usage.
steps.expected: Dedicated opentoggl_test schema should exist for validation-owned test data.
steps.observed: Schema proof shows opentoggl_test exists in the local Postgres database used by tests.

commands: psql -Atqc "select schema_name from information_schema.schemata where schema_name like 'opentoggl_test%' order by schema_name;"
evidence.network: Real Postgres-backed service-layer reads/lists exercised by go test passed for both archival and member-disable cases
evidence.schema: Database opentoggl contains dedicated schema opentoggl_test

frictions.description: Mission prompt described a shared test schema as opentoggl_test on localhost:5432, but PostgreSQL exposes database opentoggl with schema opentoggl_test instead of a database named opentoggl_test.
frictions.resolution: Verified dedicated schema existence inside database opentoggl and relied on repo tests already wired to that schema pattern.

frictions.description: No validator-owned runtime credentials were provided for direct authenticated API replay against the shared backend runtime.
frictions.resolution: Used narrow implemented Go regression coverage plus runtime reachability and schema evidence, which was the narrowest proof available within scope.

blockers.description: VAL-REG-001 remains unproven because the current UpdateTimeEntry regression does not exercise a conflicting second-start path, and mission/shared scrutiny already documents that gap.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed current implementation, scrutiny artifacts, and ran the narrow regression tests to confirm the present evidence boundary.

summary: Tested 2 assertions with narrow backend evidence: VAL-REG-003 passed via Postgres-backed regression tests; VAL-REG-001 is blocked because current repo evidence still does not prove a second conflicting start entry point beyond CreateTimeEntry.
